data.body: CONFIRMED (terraform state vs live AWS): both gaps are DEPLOYMENT drift, not code defects — the earlier 'named in infra' was correct. Gap (a) SNS: aws_sns_topic_subscription.alerts_email IS defined (monitoring.tf:49-53), endpoint /rebar/prod/alert-endpoint=joeoakhart@navapbc.com is populated, and it's in tfstate as pending_confirmation=True — but the confirmation email was never clicked and AWS auto-deleted the unconfirmed subscription after ~72h (env-or-config; the manual step at monitoring.tf:45-48 was skipped). Gap (b) 3 alarms: all defined (monitoring_1fa8.tf:44, monitoring_88ab.tf:35, monitoring_ws7.tf:25) but ABSENT from tfstate (serial 10) AND live -> terraform apply never ran against a tree containing them; no terraform CI (apply is manual per infra/runbooks/provision-restore.md:108-274) -> committed HCL and the account silently diverged (incomplete-implementation). Incidental inverse drift: rebar-gerrit-data-disk-high is live but not in HCL/state. FIX (operational, NOT code): (1) terraform apply from infra/terraform/ (plan shows 3 alarms to add + subscription re-create); (2) click the confirmation email to joeoakhart@navapbc.com so alarms actually notify; (3) add terraform to CI (drift detection / gated apply) so this can't recur. NEEDS OPERATOR — I did not mutate AWS/infra.
